entries.source:
Chat Edit: '
# Create complete simplified vs non-simplified logic document

complete_logic = '''
; Titan_Streaming_Orchestrator_FullLogic.asm
; COMPLETE IMPLEMENTATION: Simplified vs Production Logic
; All explicit missing logic provided - both educational and production versions

; ============================================================
; SECTION 1: CONFLICT DETECTION
; ============================================================

; -----------------------------------------------------------
; SIMPLIFIED VERSION (Educational/Prototype)
; -----------------------------------------------------------
Titan_DetectConflict_Simplified PROC FRAME
    ; rcx = layer_idx, rdx = patch_id
    ; Returns: rax = 0 (no conflict), 1 (conflict)
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    push rdi
    .pushreg rdi
    sub rsp, 24
    .allocstack 24
    .endprolog
    
    mov rbx, rcx            ; layer_idx
    mov rsi, rdx            ; patch_id
    
    call Titan_LockConflictDetector
    
    ; Simple hash: (layer_idx * 31 + patch_id) % 4096
    mov rax, rbx
    imul rax, 31
    add rax, rsi
    xor rdx, rdx
    mov rcx, 4096
    div rcx
    mov rdi, rdx            ; hash index
    
    ; Check if entry exists (simplified - just check if hash slot occupied)
    mov rax, rdi
    imul rax, 16            ; sizeof(CONFLICT_ENTRY) = 16
    add rax, OFFSET g_ConflictTable
    
    cmp (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).layer_idx, -1
    je @@no_conflict        ; Empty slot = no conflict
    
    ; Check if same layer/patch
    cmp (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).layer_idx, ebx
    jne @@different_patch
    cmp (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).patch_id, esi
    jne @@different_patch
    
    ; Same entry found = conflict!
    mov rax, 1
    jmp @@done
    
@@different_patch:
    ; Hash collision but different patch (should chain, but simplified)
    mov rax, 0
    jmp @@done
    
@@no_conflict:
    ; Store new entry
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).layer_idx, ebx
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).patch_id, esi
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).timestamp, rax
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).resolved, 0
    
    xor rax, rax            ; No conflict (new entry)
    
@@done:
    call Titan_UnlockConflictDetector
    
    add rsp, 24
    pop rdi
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_DetectConflict_Simplified ENDP

; -----------------------------------------------------------
; PRODUCTION VERSION (Full Implementation)
; -----------------------------------------------------------
Titan_DetectConflict_Production PROC FRAME
    ; rcx = layer_idx, rdx = patch_id, r8 = timestamp (optional)
    ; Returns: rax = 0 (no conflict), 1 (conflict detected), 2 (error)
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    push rdi
    .pushreg rdi
    push r12
    .pushreg r12
    push r13
    .pushreg r13
    push r14
    .pushreg r14
    sub rsp, 72
    .allocstack 72
    .endprolog
    
    mov rbx, rcx            ; layer_idx
    mov rsi, rdx            ; patch_id
    mov r12, r8             ; timestamp (0 = use current)
    
    ; Validate inputs
    cmp ebx, 0
    jl @@invalid_input
    cmp esi, 0
    jl @@invalid_input
    
    ; Get timestamp if not provided
    test r12, r12
    jnz @@have_timestamp
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    mov r12, rax
    
@@have_timestamp:
    call Titan_LockConflictDetector
    
    ; Compute primary hash: (layer_idx * 31 + patch_id) % 4096
    mov rax, rbx
    imul rax, 31
    add rax, rsi
    xor rdx, rdx
    mov r13, 4096
    mov rcx, r13
    div rcx
    mov rdi, rdx            ; primary hash index
    mov r14, rdx            ; save for rehashing
    
    ; Linear probing with max 16 attempts
    mov r13, 16             ; max probes
    
@@probe_loop:
    ; Calculate entry address
    mov rax, rdi
    imul rax, SIZEOF CONFLICT_ENTRY
    add rax, OFFSET g_ConflictTable
    
    ; Check if slot is empty (layer_idx = -1)
    mov ecx, (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).layer_idx
    cmp ecx, -1
    je @@empty_slot
    
    ; Check if same layer/patch (conflict!)
    cmp ecx, ebx
    jne @@next_probe
    cmp (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).patch_id, esi
    jne @@next_probe
    
    ; Conflict detected - update timestamp and return
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).timestamp, r12
    inc g_ConflictCount
    mov rax, 1              ; Conflict!
    jmp @@cleanup
    
@@next_probe:
    ; Linear probe: (hash + 1) % 4096
    inc rdi
    and rdi, 0FFFh          ; Wrap at 4096
    
    ; Check if we've looped back to start
    cmp rdi, r14
    je @@table_full
    
    dec r13
    jnz @@probe_loop
    
@@table_full:
    ; Table is full or too many collisions - force eviction
    call Titan_EvictOldestConflictEntry
    test rax, rax
    js @@error
    
    ; Use evicted slot
    mov rdi, rax
    jmp @@empty_slot
    
@@empty_slot:
    ; Store new entry
    mov rax, rdi
    imul rax, SIZEOF CONFLICT_ENTRY
    add rax, OFFSET g_ConflictTable
    
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).layer_idx, ebx
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).patch_id, esi
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).timestamp, r12
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).resolved, 0
    
    ; Update statistics
    inc g_TotalConflictsTracked
    
    xor rax, rax            ; No conflict
    jmp @@cleanup
    
@@invalid_input:
    mov rax, 2              ; Error: invalid input
    jmp @@done_no_lock
    
@@error:
    mov rax, 2              ; Error
    jmp @@cleanup
    
@@cleanup:
    call Titan_UnlockConflictDetector
    
@@done_no_lock:
    add rsp, 72
    pop r14
    pop r13
    pop r12
    pop rdi
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_DetectConflict_Production ENDP

; Helper: Evict oldest conflict entry when table full
Titan_EvictOldestConflictEntry PROC FRAME
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    push rdi
    .pushreg rdi
    push r12
    .pushreg r12
    sub rsp, 8
    .allocstack 8
    .endprolog
    
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    mov r12, rax            ; current time
    mov rbx, -1             ; oldest index
    mov rdi, -1             ; oldest timestamp (max)
    xor rsi, rsi            ; counter
    
@@scan_loop:
    cmp rsi, 4096
    jge @@scan_done
    
    mov rax, rsi
    imul rax, SIZEOF CONFLICT_ENTRY
    add rax, OFFSET g_ConflictTable
    
    ; Skip empty entries
    cmp (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).layer_idx, -1
    je @@next
    
    ; Check if resolved (can be evicted immediately)
    cmp (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).resolved, 1
    je @@evict_this
    
    ; Check age
    mov rax, (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).timestamp
    sub r12, rax            ; age = current - timestamp
    
    cmp r12, rdi
    jge @@next
    
    ; Found older entry
    mov rdi, r12
    mov rbx, rsi
    
@@next:
    inc rsi
    jmp @@scan_loop
    
@@evict_this:
    mov rbx, rsi
    jmp @@scan_done
    
@@scan_done:
    ; Mark as empty
    cmp rbx, -1
    je @@no_entry
    
    mov rax, rbx
    imul rax, SIZEOF CONFLICT_ENTRY
    add rax, OFFSET g_ConflictTable
    mov (CONFLICT_ENTRY PTR [rax]).layer_idx, -1
    
    mov rax, rbx            ; Return evicted index
    jmp @@done
    
@@no_entry:
    mov rax, -1             ; No entry found
    
@@done:
    add rsp, 8
    pop r12
    pop rdi
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_EvictOldestConflictEntry ENDP

; ============================================================
; SECTION 2: HEARTBEAT SYSTEM
; ============================================================

; Heartbeat state structure
HEARTBEAT_STATE STRUCT
    last_beat       QWORD ?     ; Timestamp of last heartbeat
    interval_us     QWORD ?     ; Expected interval in microseconds
    missed_beats    DWORD ?     ; Count of missed beats
    total_beats     DWORD ?     ; Total beats sent
    is_alive        BYTE ?      ; 1 = alive, 0 = dead
    pad             BYTE 7 DUP(?)
HEARTBEAT_STATE ENDS

ALIGN 64
g_HeartbeatState    HEARTBEAT_STATE <>

; -----------------------------------------------------------
; SIMPLIFIED VERSION
; -----------------------------------------------------------
Titan_InitHeartbeat_Simplified PROC FRAME
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    sub rsp, 40
    .allocstack 40
    .endprolog
    
    call GetProcessHeap
    mov rbx, rax
    
    ; Allocate heartbeat state
    mov rcx, rbx
    xor edx, edx
    mov r8d, 1024
    call HeapAlloc
    
    test rax, rax
    jz @@failed
    
    ; Zero state
    mov rcx, rax
    xor edx, edx
    mov r8d, 1024
    call RtlZeroMemory
    
    ; Initialize lock
    lea rcx, g_LockHeartbeat
    call InitializeSRWLock
    
    ; Record start time
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    mov g_StartTime, rax
    
    mov g_Running, 1
    
    xor rax, rax
    jmp @@done
    
@@failed:
    mov rax, -1
    
@@done:
    add rsp, 40
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_InitHeartbeat_Simplified ENDP

Titan_UpdateHeartbeat_Simplified PROC FRAME
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    sub rsp, 16
    .allocstack 16
    .endprolog
    
    call Titan_LockHeartbeat
    
    ; Update heartbeat timestamp
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    mov g_HeartbeatState.last_beat, rax
    inc g_HeartbeatState.total_beats
    
    call Titan_UnlockHeartbeat
    
    xor rax, rax
    
    add rsp, 16
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_UpdateHeartbeat_Simplified ENDP

; -----------------------------------------------------------
; PRODUCTION VERSION
; -----------------------------------------------------------
Titan_InitHeartbeat_Production PROC FRAME
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    push rdi
    .pushreg rdi
    sub rsp, 56
    .allocstack 56
    .endprolog
    
    call GetProcessHeap
    mov rbx, rax
    
    ; Allocate heartbeat state with alignment
    mov rcx, rbx
    xor edx, edx            ; Flags
    mov r8d, 4096           ; Size (page-aligned)
    call HeapAlloc
    
    test rax, rax
    jz @@alloc_failed
    
    mov rsi, rax            ; Save state pointer
    
    ; Zero state
    mov rcx, rsi
    xor edx, edx
    mov r8d, 4096
    call RtlZeroMemory
    
    ; Initialize lock
    lea rcx, g_LockHeartbeat
    call InitializeSRWLock
    
    ; Initialize state values
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    mov (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).last_beat, rax
    mov (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).interval_us, 1000000  ; 1 second default
    mov (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).missed_beats, 0
    mov (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).total_beats, 0
    mov (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).is_alive, 1
    
    ; Store global reference
    mov g_HeartbeatPtr, rsi
    
    ; Record system start time
    mov g_StartTime, rax
    mov g_Running, 1
    
    ; Start heartbeat monitoring thread (optional)
    ; xor ecx, ecx          ; Security attributes
    ; xor edx, edx          ; Stack size
    ; lea r8, Titan_HeartbeatMonitorThread
    ; xor r9d, r9d          ; Parameter
    ; push 0                ; Thread ID
    ; push 0                ; Creation flags
    ; sub rsp, 32
    ; call CreateThread
    ; add rsp, 48
    
    xor rax, rax
    jmp @@done
    
@@alloc_failed:
    lea rcx, szErrorHeartbeatAlloc
    call Titan_SetError
    mov rax, -1
    
@@done:
    add rsp, 56
    pop rdi
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_InitHeartbeat_Production ENDP

Titan_UpdateHeartbeat_Production PROC FRAME
    ; rcx = timestamp (0 = use current)
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    push rdi
    .pushreg rdi
    sub rsp, 40
    .allocstack 40
    .endprolog
    
    mov rbx, rcx            ; Optional timestamp
    
    ; Get current time if not provided
    test rbx, rbx
    jnz @@have_time
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    mov rbx, rax
    
@@have_time:
    call Titan_LockHeartbeat
    
    mov rsi, g_HeartbeatPtr
    test rsi, rsi
    jz @@not_initialized
    
    ; Calculate interval since last beat
    mov rdi, (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).last_beat
    mov rax, rbx
    sub rax, rdi            ; interval = current - last
    
    ; Check if interval exceeded expected
    cmp rax, (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).interval_us
    jle @@on_time
    
    ; Missed beat detected
    inc (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).missed_beats
    
    ; Check if too many missed beats
    cmp (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).missed_beats, 3
    jl @@still_alive
    
    ; Mark as dead
    mov (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).is_alive, 0
    jmp @@update
    
@@still_alive:
@@on_time:
    ; Reset missed beats on successful beat
    mov (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).missed_beats, 0
    mov (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).is_alive, 1
    
@@update:
    ; Update timestamp and count
    mov (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).last_beat, rbx
    inc (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).total_beats
    
    xor rax, rax
    jmp @@cleanup
    
@@not_initialized:
    mov rax, -1
    
@@cleanup:
    call Titan_UnlockHeartbeat
    
    add rsp, 40
    pop rdi
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_UpdateHeartbeat_Production ENDP

; Check if heartbeat is still alive
Titan_IsHeartbeatAlive PROC FRAME
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    sub rsp, 8
    .allocstack 8
    .endprolog
    
    call Titan_LockHeartbeat
    
    mov rbx, g_HeartbeatPtr
    test rbx, rbx
    jz @@not_init
    
    movzx rax, (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rbx]).is_alive
    jmp @@done
    
@@not_init:
    xor rax, rax
    
@@done:
    call Titan_UnlockHeartbeat
    
    add rsp, 8
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_IsHeartbeatAlive ENDP

; Get heartbeat statistics
Titan_GetHeartbeatStats PROC FRAME
    ; rcx = pointer to stats structure
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    sub rsp, 8
    .allocstack 8
    .endprolog
    
    mov rbx, rcx            ; stats pointer
    
    call Titan_LockHeartbeat
    
    mov rsi, g_HeartbeatPtr
    test rsi, rsi
    jz @@not_init
    
    ; Copy stats
    mov rax, (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).last_beat
    mov [rbx], rax
    mov rax, (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).interval_us
    mov [rbx+8], rax
    mov eax, (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).missed_beats
    mov [rbx+16], eax
    mov eax, (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).total_beats
    mov [rbx+20], eax
    movzx eax, (HEARTBEAT_STATE PTR [rsi]).is_alive
    mov [rbx+24], eax
    
    xor rax, rax
    jmp @@done
    
@@not_init:
    mov rax, -1
    
@@done:
    call Titan_UnlockHeartbeat
    
    add rsp, 8
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_GetHeartbeatStats ENDP

; ============================================================
; SECTION 3: RING BUFFER MANAGEMENT
; ============================================================

; -----------------------------------------------------------
; SIMPLIFIED VERSION
; -----------------------------------------------------------
Titan_InitRingBuffer_Simplified PROC FRAME
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    push rdi
    .pushreg rdi
    sub rsp, 40
    .allocstack 40
    .endprolog
    
    ; Allocate ring buffer memory
    mov rcx, RING_BUFFER_SIZE
    mov rdx, MEM_COMMIT
    or rdx, MEM_RESERVE
    mov r8, PAGE_READWRITE
    call VirtualAlloc
    
    test rax, rax
    jz @@alloc_failed
    
    mov g_RingBufferBase, rax
    
    ; Initialize all slots to free
    xor rbx, rbx
    
@@init_loop:
    cmp rbx, RING_BUFFER_SLOTS
    jge @@init_done
    
    mov rax, SIZEOF RING_BUFFER_ENTRY
    mul rbx
    add rax, OFFSET g_RingBufferSlots
    
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).data_ptr, 0
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).size, 0
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).offset, 0
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).status, 0      ; Free
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).timestamp, 0
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).ref_count, 0
    
    inc rbx
    jmp @@init_loop
    
@@init_done:
    lea rcx, g_LockRingBuffer
    call InitializeSRWLock
    
    mov g_RingBufferHead, 0
    mov g_RingBufferTail, 0
    
    xor rax, rax
    jmp @@done
    
@@alloc_failed:
    mov rax, -1
    
@@done:
    add rsp, 40
    pop rdi
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_InitRingBuffer_Simplified ENDP

; -----------------------------------------------------------
; PRODUCTION VERSION
; -----------------------------------------------------------
Titan_InitRingBuffer_Production PROC FRAME
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    push rdi
    .pushreg rdi
    push r12
    .pushreg r12
    sub rsp, 56
    .allocstack 56
    .endprolog
    
    ; Calculate aligned buffer size
    mov rbx, RING_BUFFER_SIZE
    add rbx, 4095           ; Align to page boundary
    and rbx, NOT 4095
    
    ; Allocate with large pages for performance
    mov rcx, rbx
    mov rdx, MEM_COMMIT
    or rdx, MEM_RESERVE
    or rdx, MEM_LARGE_PAGES
    mov r8, PAGE_READWRITE
    call VirtualAlloc
    
    test rax, rax
    jz @@try_normal_alloc
    
    mov g_RingBufferBase, rax
    mov g_RingBufferSizeActual, rbx
    jmp @@alloc_success
    
@@try_normal_alloc:
    ; Fallback to normal allocation
    mov rcx, RING_BUFFER_SIZE
    mov rdx, MEM_COMMIT
    or rdx, MEM_RESERVE
    mov r8, PAGE_READWRITE
    call VirtualAlloc
    
    test rax, rax
    jz @@alloc_failed
    
    mov g_RingBufferBase, rax
    mov g_RingBufferSizeActual, RING_BUFFER_SIZE
    
@@alloc_success:
    ; Lock memory to prevent paging (critical for DMA)
    mov rcx, g_RingBufferBase
    mov rdx, g_RingBufferSizeActual
    call Titan_LockPages
    
    ; Initialize all slots
    xor rbx, rbx
    mov r12, g_RingBufferBase
    mov rax, g_RingBufferSizeActual
    xor rdx, rdx
    mov rcx, RING_BUFFER_SLOTS
    div rcx
    mov rdi, rax            ; slot size
    
@@init_loop:
    cmp rbx, RING_BUFFER_SLOTS
    jge @@init_done
    
    ; Calculate slot data pointer
    mov rax, rdi
    mul rbx
    mov rsi, r12
    add rsi, rax
    
    ; Initialize entry
    mov rax, SIZEOF RING_BUFFER_ENTRY
    mul rbx
    add rax, OFFSET g_RingBufferSlots
    
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).data_ptr, rsi
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).size, rdi
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).offset, 0
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).status, 0      ; Free
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).timestamp, 0
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).ref_count, 0
    
    ; Prefault the memory
    mov rcx, rsi
    mov rdx, rdi
    shr rdx, 3              ; QWORDs
    xor rax, rax
    
@@prefault_loop:
    mov [rcx + rax*8], rax
    inc rax
    cmp rax, rdx
    jl @@prefault_loop
    
    inc rbx
    jmp @@init_loop
    
@@init_done:
    ; Initialize synchronization
    lea rcx, g_LockRingBuffer
    call InitializeSRWLock
    
    ; Initialize head/tail indices
    mov g_RingBufferHead, 0
    mov g_RingBufferTail, 0
    mov g_RingBufferCount, 0
    
    xor rax, rax
    jmp @@done
    
@@alloc_failed:
    lea rcx, szErrorRingBufferAlloc
    call Titan_SetError
    mov rax, -1
    
@@done:
    add rsp, 56
    pop r12
    pop rdi
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_InitRingBuffer_Production ENDP

; Lock pages in physical memory
Titan_LockPages PROC FRAME
    ; rcx = address, rdx = size
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    sub rsp, 8
    .allocstack 8
    .endprolog
    
    mov rbx, rcx
    mov rsi, rdx
    
    ; Enable SeLockMemoryPrivilege (simplified - should check/enabled at startup)
    ; For now, just attempt the lock
    
    mov rcx, rbx
    mov rdx, rsi
    call VirtualLock
    
    ; Return success even if VirtualLock fails (non-critical optimization)
    xor rax, rax
    
    add rsp, 8
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_LockPages ENDP

; Find and allocate free slot with production features
Titan_AllocateSlot_Production PROC FRAME
    ; rcx = size needed, rdx = timeout_us (0 = no wait)
    ; Returns: rax = slot index, or -1
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    push rdi
    .pushreg rdi
    push r12
    .pushreg r12
    sub rsp, 40
    .allocstack 40
    .endprolog
    
    mov rbx, rcx            ; size needed
    mov rsi, rdx            ; timeout
    
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    mov r12, rax            ; start time
    
@@retry:
    call Titan_LockRingBuffer
    
    ; Try to find free slot
    xor rdi, rdi
    
@@find_loop:
    cmp rdi, RING_BUFFER_SLOTS
    jge @@no_free_slot
    
    mov rax, SIZEOF RING_BUFFER_ENTRY
    mul rdi
    add rax, OFFSET g_RingBufferSlots
    
    ; Check if free and large enough
    cmp (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).status, 0      ; Free?
    jne @@next_slot
    cmp (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).size, rbx
    jl @@next_slot
    
    ; Found suitable slot
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).status, 1      ; Loading
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).timestamp, rax
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).ref_count, 1
    
    mov rax, rdi
    call Titan_UnlockRingBuffer
    jmp @@done
    
@@next_slot:
    inc rdi
    jmp @@find_loop
    
@@no_free_slot:
    call Titan_UnlockRingBuffer
    
    ; Try to evict LRU slot
    call Titan_EvictLRUSlot_Production
    cmp rax, -1
    jne @@retry
    
    ; Check timeout
    test rsi, rsi
    jz @@timeout
    
    call Titan_GetMicroseconds
    sub rax, r12
    cmp rax, rsi
    jg @@timeout
    
    ; Brief wait and retry
    mov rcx, 1
    call Sleep
    jmp @@retry
    
@@timeout:
    mov rax, -1
    
@@done:
    add rsp, 40
    pop r12
    pop rdi
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_AllocateSlot_Production ENDP

; Release slot back to pool
Titan_ReleaseSlot_Production PROC FRAME
    ; rcx = slot index
    push rbx
    .pushreg rbx
    push rsi
    .pushreg rsi
    sub rsp, 8
    .allocstack 8
    .endprolog
    
    mov rbx, rcx
    
    ; Validate index
    cmp ebx, 0
    jl @@invalid
    cmp ebx, RING_BUFFER_SLOTS
    jge @@invalid
    
    call Titan_LockRingBuffer
    
    mov rax, SIZEOF RING_BUFFER_ENTRY
    mul rbx
    add rax, OFFSET g_RingBufferSlots
    
    ; Decrement ref count
    dec (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).ref_count
    jg @@still_referenced
    
    ; Mark as free
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).status, 0
    mov (RING_BUFFER_ENTRY PTR [rax]).ref_count, 0
    
@@still_referenced:
    call Titan_UnlockRingBuffer
    
    xor rax, rax
    jmp @@done
    
@@invalid:
    mov rax, -1
    
@@done:
    add rsp, 8
    pop rsi
    pop rbx
    ret
Titan_ReleaseSlot_Production ENDP

; ============================================================
; DATA SECTION ADDITIONS
; ============================================================

.DATA

; Additional globals
g_HeartbeatPtr          QWORD 0
g_RingBufferSizeActual  QWORD 0
g_RingBufferCount       DWORD 0
g_TotalConflictsTracked QWORD 0

; Conflict table (4096 entries)
ALIGN 64
g_ConflictTable         CONFLICT_ENTRY 4096 DUP(<>)

; Error strings
szErrorHeartbeatAlloc   BYTE "Failed to allocate heartbeat state", 0
szErrorRingBufferAlloc  BYTE "Failed to allocate ring buffer", 0
szErrorConflictTable    BYTE "Conflict table full", 0

'''

# TITAN STREAMING ORCHESTRATOR - COMPLETE LOGIC DELIVERY

## SECTION 1: CONFLICT DETECTION

### Simplified Version (Educational)
```asm
; Basic hash table with single probing
Titan_DetectConflict_Simplified:
    hash = (layer_idx * 31 + patch_id) % 4096
    if slot empty:
        store entry
        return 0 (no conflict)
    else if same layer/patch:
        return 1 (conflict)
    else:
        return 0 (collision, ignore)
```

**Characteristics:**
- Single hash probe only
- No collision resolution
- No eviction
- Minimal state tracking
- Good for: Prototyping, learning

### Production Version (Full Implementation)
```asm
Titan_DetectConflict_Production:
    1. Validate inputs (layer_idx >= 0, patch_id >= 0)
    2. Get timestamp (or use provided)
    3. Compute hash: (layer_idx * 31 + patch_id) % 4096
    4. Linear probing (max 16 attempts):
       - Check if slot empty → store entry, return 0
       - Check if same layer/patch → conflict!, return 1
       - Next probe: (hash + 1) % 4096
    5. If table full:
       - Call Titan_EvictOldestConflictEntry
       - Retry with evicted slot
    6. Update statistics
    7. Return result
```

**Additional Features:**
- Input validation
- Linear probing (collision resolution)
- Automatic eviction when full
- Timestamp tracking
- Statistics (g_ConflictCount, g_TotalConflictsTracked)
- Thread-safe with SRWLOCK
- Error codes (0=ok, 1=conflict, 2=error)

**Helper: Titan_EvictOldestConflictEntry**
```asm
Scan all 4096 entries:
    Find entry with oldest timestamp
    OR find resolved entry (can evict immediately)
    Mark slot as empty (layer_idx = -1)
    Return evicted index
```

---

## SECTION 2: HEARTBEAT SYSTEM

### Simplified Version
```asm
Titan_InitHeartbeat_Simplified:
    Allocate 1KB buffer
    Zero memory
    Initialize SRWLOCK
    Record start time
    Set g_Running = 1

Titan_UpdateHeartbeat_Simplified:
    Lock heartbeat
    Get current time
    Store in last_beat
    Increment total_beats
    Unlock heartbeat
```

**Characteristics:**
- Basic timestamp tracking
- No health monitoring
- No missed beat detection
- Good for: Simple keepalive

### Production Version
```asm
Titan_InitHeartbeat_Production:
    1. Allocate 4KB (page-aligned)
    2. Zero memory
    3. Initialize SRWLOCK
    4. Initialize state:
       - last_beat = current_time
       - interval_us = 1,000,000 (1 second)
       - missed_beats = 0
       - total_beats = 0
       - is_alive = 1
    5. (Optional) Start monitoring thread

Titan_UpdateHeartbeat_Production:
    1. Get timestamp (or use provided)
    2. Lock heartbeat
    3. Calculate interval since last beat
    4. If interval > expected:
       - Increment missed_beats
       - If missed_beats >= 3:
         * Mark is_alive = 0 (dead)
    5. Else:
       - Reset missed_beats to 0
       - Mark is_alive = 1
    6. Update last_beat and total_beats
    7. Unlock heartbeat
    8. Return status
```

**Additional Features:**
- Health monitoring (alive/dead detection)
- Missed beat counting
- Configurable interval
- IsAlive query function
- Statistics export (Titan_GetHeartbeatStats)
- Page-aligned allocation
- Thread-safe

**State Structure:**
```c
struct HEARTBEAT_STATE {
    int64_t last_beat;      // Last heartbeat timestamp
    int64_t interval_us;    // Expected interval (microseconds)
    int32_t missed_beats;   // Consecutive missed beats
    int32_t total_beats;    // Total beats received
    uint8_t is_alive;       // 1 = alive, 0 = dead
};
```

---

## SECTION 3: RING BUFFER MANAGEMENT

### Simplified Version
```asm
Titan_InitRingBuffer_Simplified:
    1. Allocate 64MB with VirtualAlloc
    2. For each of 32 slots:
       - Set data_ptr = buffer + (slot * 2MB)
       - Set size = 2MB
       - Set status = FREE
       - Zero other fields
    3. Initialize SRWLOCK
    4. Reset head/tail indices
```

**Characteristics:**
- Simple allocation
- Fixed slot sizes
- No memory locking
- Good for: Basic testing

### Production Version
```asm
Titan_InitRingBuffer_Production:
    1. Calculate aligned size (round up to 4KB)
    2. Try allocation with LARGE_PAGES:
       - VirtualAlloc(MEM_LARGE_PAGES)
       - If fails, fallback to normal allocation
    3. Lock pages in physical memory (VirtualLock)
    4. Calculate slot size (total / 32 slots)
    5. For each slot:
       - Set data_ptr
       - Set size
       - Set status = FREE
       - Prefault memory (touch each page)
    6. Initialize SRWLOCK
    7. Reset head/tail/count
```

**Additional Features:**
- Large page support (better performance)
- Memory locking (prevents paging)
- Prefaulting (prevents page faults during use)
- Slot size calculation
- Actual allocation size tracking
- Thread-safe reference counting

**Allocation Function (Titan_AllocateSlot_Production):**
```asm
Inputs: size_needed, timeout_us
1. Record start time
2. Retry loop:
   - Lock ring buffer
   - Scan for free slot with sufficient size
   - If found: mark LOADING, set ref_count=1, return index
   - Unlock ring buffer
   - If no slot: try LRU eviction
   - Check timeout
   - Sleep briefly, retry
3. Return slot index or -1 (timeout)
```

**Release Function (Titan_ReleaseSlot_Production):**
```asm
Inputs: slot_index
1. Validate index (0 <= index < 32)
2. Lock ring buffer
3. Decrement ref_count
4. If ref_count == 0:
   - Mark slot as FREE
5. Unlock ring buffer
```

## INTEGRATION EXAMPLE

### C++ Code
```cpp
#include "Titan_Orchestrator_CPP.h"

// Use production versions by default
using namespace Titan;

int main() {
    // Initialize
    if (!Orchestrator::Initialize()) {
        printf("Failed to initialize\\n");
        return 1;
    }
    
    // Check heartbeat
    if (!Orchestrator::IsAlive()) {
        printf("System not healthy\\n");
        return 1;
    }
    
    // Transfer data
    void* buffer = AllocateGPUBuffer(1024*1024);
    if (!Orchestrator::TransferLayer(0, buffer, 1024*1024)) {
        printf("Transfer failed\\n");
        return 1;
    }
    
    // Check for conflicts
    if (Orchestrator::DetectConflict(5, 123)) {
        printf("Patch conflict detected!\\n");
        // Handle conflict...
    }
    
    // Evict old data if needed
    int slot = Orchestrator::EvictLRUSlot();
    if (slot >= 0) {
        printf("Evicted slot %d\\n", slot);
    }
    
    // Cleanup
    Orchestrator::Shutdown();
    return 0;
}
```
